bio: Kevin Muniz is a 24 year old Computer Science student at the University of Central Florida, graduating in May 2026. He has hands-on experience in software engineering, systems engineering, and AI-driven applications. He has worked at Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits, Lockheed Martin, and Apple, where he built scalable systems, improved engineering workflows, and contributed to mission-critical technologies. Kevin specializes in full-stack development, mobile app development, AI and Machine Learning, finance applications, and business analytics.
general_summary: Kevin Muniz is a 24 year old software engineer and Computer Science student at the University of Central Florida graduating in May 2026. He has professional experience at three companies — Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits where he currently works as an OneTech Data Intern, Lockheed Martin where he worked as both a Systems Engineer Intern and a Full Stack Software Engineer Intern building internal tools and working on F-35 and F-16 targeting systems, and Apple where he worked through the Apple College Program managing 75 or more weekly technical cases with a 92% satisfaction rate. Kevin is skilled in Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C, C#, Swift, and SQL. He builds with React, React Native, Node.js, Express, Next.js, FastAPI, Spring Boot, and more. He is experienced with Docker, AWS, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Git, and machine learning tools like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Scikit-learn. His projects include LEMA a cross-platform energy monitoring app, an AI-enhanced paper trading platform, a secure payment network, and a transformer language model built from scratch. Outside of work Kevin is passionate about fitness, snowboarding, hiking, cooking, traveling, and music. He is outgoing, collaborative, and looking for full-time software engineering roles where he can grow alongside a great team.
work_history_summary: Kevin has worked at three companies: Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits, Lockheed Martin, and Apple. He is currently working at Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits as an OneTech Data Intern. Before that he had two roles at Lockheed Martin — first as a Full Stack Software Engineer Intern from September 2024 to May 2025, then as a Systems Engineer Intern starting May 2025. Before Lockheed Martin he worked at Apple from March 2023 to September 2024 through the Apple College Program AHA. His background spans software engineering, systems engineering, data, full-stack web development, and technical support.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

experience.description: Kevin is currently working at Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits as an OneTech Data Intern. Southern Glazer's is the largest wine and spirits distributor in the United States. In this role Kevin works within the technology and data division contributing to data-driven initiatives and internal tooling.

Kevin worked at Lockheed Martin as a Systems Engineer Intern. He gathered requirements for land and air-based targeting systems including the F-35 and F-16 fighter jets and created detailed technical documentation to improve process understanding across engineering teams. He developed onboarding guides and reference materials that cut new engineer ramp-up time by 25% and standardized knowledge transfer across projects. He also conducted research on communication networks between legacy and newly developed systems, reducing miscommunication errors and increasing cross-platform reliability.
impact: Reduced new engineer onboarding time by 25% through comprehensive documentation and onboarding guides
Improved cross-platform communication reliability by researching interactions between legacy and modern systems
Worked on F-35 and F-16 land and air-based targeting systems

experience.description: Kevin worked at Lockheed Martin as a Full Stack Software Engineer Intern. He developed full-stack internal web applications using C#, Razor, and SQL through automated data pipelines. He built and deployed a scalable datagrid system to organize and visualize user data, streamline resource allocation, and improve turnaround time on mission-critical reports by over 30%. He also reduced manual data entry by 40% by integrating backend logic with existing databases.
impact: Improved mission-critical report turnaround time by over 30% with a scalable datagrid visualization system
Reduced manual data entry by 40% through backend automation and database integration
Built full-stack internal tools using C#, Razor, and SQL

experience.description: Kevin worked at Apple through the Apple College Program AHA. He resolved user cases in Apple's CORE system, managing 75 or more weekly issues and maintaining a 92% customer satisfaction rate. He created and maintained detailed technical case notes for senior engineers improving issue traceability. He also provided mentorship and career guidance to peers, contributing to multiple students earning Apple internships.
impact: Managed 75+ weekly cases with a 92% customer satisfaction rate
Improved issue traceability through structured technical case documentation for senior engineers
Mentored peers resulting in multiple students earning Apple internships

projects.name: Lightweight Energy Meter Aggregator (LEMA)
projects.description: LEMA is a cross-platform mobile app Kevin built to cut facility energy monitoring costs by up to 80%, enabling scalable deployment for buildings over 5,000 square feet without requiring $50k to $100k enterprise systems. Kevin implemented LAN-based meter polling with offline data caching achieving 95%+ uptime and full functionality without internet access. He also built modular reporting tools with CSV, XLSX, and PDF export and interactive dashboards that reduce manual reporting time by 70% and process 1,500 or more daily data points per facility.
details: Reduces facility energy monitoring costs by up to 80% compared to $50k–$100k enterprise systems
LAN-based meter polling with offline caching achieving 95%+ uptime without internet connectivity
Processes 1,500+ daily data points per facility with CSV/XLSX/PDF export and interactive dashboards

projects.name: AI-Enhanced Paper Trading Platform
projects.description: Kevin built a full-stack MERN trading simulator to execute, monitor, and analyze simulated trades in real time with sub-100 millisecond transaction latency. He integrated the OpenAI API for large-scale market sentiment analysis enabling users to derive actionable trading signals from financial news and social media data. He containerized backend microservices with Docker to ensure scalable deployment, fault tolerance, and consistent API performance under concurrent user load.
details: Sub-100ms transaction latency for real-time paper trading simulation
OpenAI API integration for market sentiment analysis from financial news and social media
Dockerized microservices for scalable and fault-tolerant deployment

projects.name: Secure Payment Network
projects.description: Kevin built a secure payment backend using Spring Boot and PostgreSQL supporting deposits and peer-to-peer transfers. He implemented JWT authentication, HTTPS encryption, and ACID-compliant transaction handling to ensure data security and integrity. The project is currently in active development.
details: Supports deposits and peer-to-peer transfers with ACID-compliant transaction handling
JWT authentication and HTTPS encryption for data security
Built with Spring Boot and PostgreSQL

projects.name: Transformer Language Model
projects.description: Kevin built a transformer-based language model from scratch in Python, implementing attention mechanisms and sequence modeling without using pre-built model libraries. He trained the model on custom datasets to generate coherent text and explored performance tuning and optimization techniques.
details: Built attention mechanisms and sequence modeling from scratch
Trained on custom datasets to generate coherent text

leadership.description: Kevin served as Treasurer of Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity at UCF. He optimized the fraternity budget by conducting a full audit of recurring expenses, cutting unnecessary costs by 25% and reallocating resources toward high-impact academic and professional events. He generated comprehensive monthly financial reports for the executive board with visual breakdowns and year-over-year analyses. He also designed and led a weekly financial literacy program covering budgeting, credit management, investing basics, and post-graduate financial planning.
impact: Cut unnecessary costs by 25% through a full financial audit and budget optimization
Delivered monthly financial reports with visual breakdowns to the executive board
Designed and led a weekly financial literacy program for fraternity members

personality.about: Kevin is naturally outgoing and enjoys talking with people about just about anything, whether it is work, ideas, or everyday life. He loves collaborating and bouncing ideas around to create things that can actually be built and brought to life. While he likes to joke around and keep things fun, he is also able to switch gears quickly and stay focused when it is time to get serious and execute.
hobbies: Kevin is passionate about fitness and enjoys working out regularly to maintain a disciplined and healthy lifestyle. He lifts and runs every day.
He enjoys snowboarding, hiking, and almost any outdoor activity.
Kevin enjoys cooking for others whenever he can. It is one of the ways he shows that he cares.
interests_extended: Kevin is really interested in listening to all kinds of music but his favorite genres are house, rock and indie pop.
He enjoys traveling a lot. Paris, France is his current favorite place he has visited. His dream destination is Japan.
favorite_foods: Kevin enjoys high-protein meals that support his fitness goals, including chicken, steak, and rice-based dishes.
He likes trying new foods and restaurants, especially meals that balance flavor and nutrition.
His favorite foods are sushi and Italian and Latin food.
fun_facts: Kevin has been skydiving. It was really scary but so much more fun.
The only food Kevin does not like is olives.
Kevin is the second engineer in his family.

goals: Kevin is seeking full-time roles involving software engineering that allow him to grow and become the best engineer he could be while working alongside an amazing team that feels like family.
